Spain enters as the model's strong favorite, with a 71% win probability that reflects a substantial advantage over Uruguay's 8% chance. The gap widens further when comparing the headline outcomes: Spain's favored status outpaces the underdog by roughly 63 percentage points.

The match shows meaningful separation across the draw line. While a draw carries 21% probability—a non-trivial share—the model concentrates most mass on an outright Spanish victory. This distribution suggests a competitive encounter with material two-way risk, though a Spain win remains the overwhelmingly likeliest single outcome.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet for this fixture. Once market odds appear, the dashboard will compare model and market assessments to identify any material divergence in the probability landscape.
